supplementEntry.name: Cistanche
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Multiple small-to-moderate RCTs (cognitive n=100, CFS n=190, locomotive n=26, muscle n=48) but most used combination products (with Ginkgo biloba); no large monotherapy RCT or meta-analysis of RCTs yet

cognitive: Cognitive function improvement in aging populations
Memory stabilization in moderate Alzheimer's disease

mechanismsOfAction: Neuroprotection via echinacoside-mediated BDNF and GDNF upregulation
Anti-amyloid activity through enhanced amyloid-beta clearance and cholinergic modulation
Antioxidant defense via Nrf2/PPARgamma signaling pathway activation
Anti-inflammatory signaling through IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 pathway inhibition
Mitochondrial protection and autophagy regulation via Sirt1 pathway
Skeletal muscle preservation through anti-atrophy and anti-sarcopenic pathways

drugInteractions: Theoretical interaction with cholinesterase inhibitors (shared pathway)
No clinically significant drug interactions reported in trials

effectSizes.cognitiveFunction: Significant MMSE improvement over 90 days in combination with Ginkgo biloba (n=100 RCT)
effectSizes.alzheimerStabilization: ADAS-cog improvement in 10/18 patients over 48 weeks (open-label monotherapy)
effectSizes.chronicFatigueSymptoms: Significant improvement in memory, physical fatigue, sleep quality, and post-exertional malaise (p<0.001, n=190 RCT)
effectSizes.locomotiveSyndrome: Improved walking speed and stand-up test performance in adults >60 years over 12 weeks

keyCitations.title: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of Cistanche tubulosa and Ginkgo biloba extracts for the improvement of cognitive function in middle-aged and elderly people
An open-label, nonplacebo-controlled study on Cistanche tubulosa glycoside capsules (Memoregain) for treating moderate Alzheimer's Disease
keyCitations.journal: American Journal of Alzheimer's Disease and Other Dementias

keyCitations.title: A Botanical Product Containing Cistanche and Ginkgo Extracts Potentially Improves Chronic Fatigue Syndrome Symptoms in Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, and Placebo-Controlled Study
Effects of Cistanche tubulosa Wight Extract on Locomotive Syndrome: A Placebo-Controlled, Randomized, Double-Blind Study

mechanisms.mechanism: Neuroprotection via echinacoside-mediated neurotrophic factor upregulation

evidence.title: Therapeutic Potential and Molecular Mechanisms of Echinacoside in Neurodegenerative Diseases
evidence.findings: Echinacoside exhibits neuroprotective effects through multiple pathways including protecting mitochondrial function, anti-oxidative stress, anti-inflammatory activity, anti-endoplasmic reticulum stress, and autophagy regulation. Upregulates BDNF and GDNF expression in Parkinson's disease models.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of preclinical and mechanistic studies on echinacoside in neurodegenerative diseases

evidence.title: An open-label, nonplacebo-controlled study on Cistanche tubulosa glycoside capsules (Memoregain) for treating moderate Alzheimer's Disease
evidence.journal: American Journal of Alzheimer's Disease and Other Dementias
evidence.findings: Cistanche tubulosa glycoside capsules (Memoregain, 300 mg/day) stabilized cognitive function in moderate AD patients over 48 weeks. ADAS-cog improved in 10/18 patients. No obvious aggravation of cognitive function, daily living ability, or overall conditions observed.
evidence.methodology: Open-label, nonplacebo-controlled study with ADAS-cog, MMSE, ADL, and CGI assessments

mechanisms.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory signaling via IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 pathway inhibition

evidence.title: Therapeutic Potential and Molecular Mechanisms of Echinacoside in Neurodegenerative Diseases
evidence.findings: Echinacoside inhibits IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 pathway, decreasing phosphorylation of STAT3 on tyr705 while increasing p-STAT3 (ser727) and BDNF expression in PD models. Demonstrates anti-neuroinflammatory effects in both in vivo and in vitro systems.
evidence.methodology: Systematic review of molecular mechanism studies

benefits.specificClaim: Improves cognitive function in middle-aged and elderly adults when combined with Ginkgo biloba
benefits.replicationStatus: Replicated across two independent RCTs plus fMRI mechanistic study

evidence.title: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of Cistanche tubulosa and Ginkgo biloba extracts for the improvement of cognitive function in middle-aged and elderly people
evidence.findings: Combination of Cistanche tubulosa (72 mg echinacoside) and Ginkgo biloba (27 mg flavonol glycosides) significantly improved MMSE scores and memory performance including long-term and delayed memory in middle-aged and elderly participants. No treatment-related adverse events reported.
evidence.methodology: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial with cognitive assessments at baseline, Day 45, and Day 90

evidence.title: Cistanche tubulosa-Ginkgo biloba combination enhances memory via cortico-cerebellar reorganization: a randomized controlled trial
evidence.studyType: Randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial with neuroimaging
evidence.findings: Cistanche tubulosa-Ginkgo biloba combination enhanced memory performance via cortico-cerebellar reorganization as demonstrated by functional MRI. Gray matter volume and functional connectivity changes observed post-intervention in healthy participants aged 30-65.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT with clinical memory assessments, structural MRI, and functional connectivity analysis

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces chronic fatigue syndrome symptoms including impaired memory, physical fatigue, and unrefreshing sleep
benefits.tissueTarget: Central nervous system and musculoskeletal system
benefits.target: Central nervous system and musculoskeletal system

evidence.title: A Botanical Product Containing Cistanche and Ginkgo Extracts Potentially Improves Chronic Fatigue Syndrome Symptoms in Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, and Placebo-Controlled Study
evidence.findings: Low dose (300 mg Cistanche + 120 mg Ginkgo) and high dose (450 mg Cistanche + 180 mg Ginkgo) both significantly improved CFS symptoms including impaired memory/concentration, physical fatigue, unrefreshing sleep, and post-exertional malaise (p<0.001) vs placebo. Improvements measured by Chalder Fatigue Scale and WHOQOL.
evidence.methodology: Three-arm RCT (low dose, high dose, placebo) in 190 adults aged 35-60 with CFS over 60 days

benefits.specificClaim: Attenuates locomotive syndrome and supports muscle strength in older adults
benefits.replicationStatus: Two independent RCTs in different populations

evidence.title: Effects of Cistanche tubulosa Wight Extract on Locomotive Syndrome: A Placebo-Controlled, Randomized, Double-Blind Study
evidence.findings: Cistanche tubulosa extract improved walking speed and stand-up test performance in older adults with pre-symptomatic or mild locomotive syndrome. Greatest improvements seen in participants aged >60 years. No adverse effects during 180-day administration at 1800 mg/subject/day.
evidence.methodology: Placebo-controlled, randomized, double-blind study with physical assessments including muscle mass, grip strength, walking speed, and stand-up test

evidence.title: Effects of Cistanche deserticola Y.C. Ma Supplementation on Muscle Strength and Recovery: A Randomized Controlled Trial
evidence.findings: Cistanche deserticola extract (5 g twice daily) evaluated in 48 males stratified by training status during 8-week resistance training program. Assessed 1RM bench press and squat, MVIC, repetitions to failure, and blood biomarkers (testosterone, cortisol, CRP, creatine kinase).
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT with resistance training protocol in trained and untrained males

safety.claim: Good safety profile supported by genotoxicity, repeated-dose toxicity testing, and clinical trial safety data

evidence.title: Safety assessment of the Cistanche tubulosa health food product Memoregain: Genotoxicity and 28-day repeated dose toxicity test
evidence.findings: No genotoxicity in Ames test (up to 5 mg/plate), no chromosomal aberrations in CHO-K1 cells, no micronuclei formation in ICR mice. 28-day repeated oral dosing (up to 0.5 g/kg body weight) in rats showed no observable adverse effects. Supported safety of Memoregain for human consumption.
evidence.methodology: Comprehensive toxicological battery including Ames test, chromosomal aberration assay, micronucleus test, and 28-day repeated dose toxicity study

evidence.title: Effects of Cistanche tubulosa Wight Extract on Locomotive Syndrome: A Placebo-Controlled, Randomized, Double-Blind Study
evidence.findings: CT extract showed no acute toxicity in mice, no chronic toxicity in rats, and no evidence of gene mutagenesis. Clinical safety assessment revealed no adverse effects during 180-day administration at 1800 mg/subject/day.
evidence.methodology: Preclinical toxicity battery plus 12-week human clinical safety monitoring

dosage.dosageRange: 300-450 mg/day standardized extract (72 mg echinacoside) or 300 mg/day Memoregain glycosides for cognitive indications
dosage.claim: Dose range established across multiple clinical trials for cognitive and anti-fatigue endpoints

evidence.title: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of Cistanche tubulosa and Ginkgo biloba extracts for the improvement of cognitive function in middle-aged and elderly people
evidence.findings: Two tablets daily providing 72 mg echinacoside and 27 mg flavonol glycosides over 90 days produced significant cognitive improvement with excellent tolerability.

evidence.title: A Botanical Product Containing Cistanche and Ginkgo Extracts Potentially Improves Chronic Fatigue Syndrome Symptoms in Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, and Placebo-Controlled Study
evidence.findings: Both low dose (300 mg Cistanche) and high dose (450 mg Cistanche) produced significant improvements in CFS symptoms, with both doses achieving p<0.001 vs placebo.
